name: size_zero_vacuously_true
reason: If aligned(addr, 0) holds for any addr, the spec is unsound on degenerate size=0 input (division by zero)

name: trivially_true_for_all
reason: If aligned is true for all addr and size, the spec is vacuous and provides no alignment guarantees

name: successor_preserves_page_alignment
code:
proof fn phi_3_successor_preserves_page_alignment(addr: nat)
    requires
        aligned(addr, PAGE_SIZE as nat),
    ensures
        aligned((addr + 1) as nat, PAGE_SIZE as nat),
{
}
reason: Adding 1 to a page-aligned address must NOT remain page-aligned; if it does the spec conflates all addresses

name: odd_is_even_aligned
reason: 1 is not a multiple of 2; if aligned(1,2) holds the modular arithmetic in the spec is broken

name: double_size_always_entailed
code:
proof fn phi_5_double_size_always_entailed(addr: nat, s: nat)
    requires
        s > 0,
        aligned(addr, s),
    ensures
        aligned(addr, s * 2),
{
}
reason: Alignment to s does not imply alignment to 2*s (e.g. 4096 is 4096-aligned but not 8192-aligned); provability would mean the spec over-generalizes
